title: Area of rectangles with fraction side lengths
description: Practice computing the areas of rectangles that have fractional side lengths.
topic_path: Area of rectangles with fractional side lengths
Area of rectangles with fraction side lengths

This can also be written as $2\dfrac{7}{9} \text{ m}^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the area of a square with $2\dfrac1{2}\text{cm}$ sidelengths?** 

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^2$
hints:
The ${\maroonD{\text{width}}}$ is $\maroonD{2\dfrac12}\,\text{cm}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can rewrite $\maroonD{2\dfrac12}$ as $\maroonD{\dfrac52}$.

The ${\blueE{\text{length}}}$ is $\blueE{2\dfrac12}\,\text{cm}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

We can rewrite $\blueE{2\dfrac12}$ as $\blueE{\dfrac52}$.

The area of the square is $\dfrac{25}{4}\text{cm}^2$.

This can also be written as $6\dfrac{1}{4}\text{cm}^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sammy has a rectangular doormat on her porch. The mat is $\dfrac45\text{ m}$ long and $\dfrac12\text{ m}$ wide. 

**What is the area of the doormat?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}^2$
hints:
Sammy's doormat is a rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Area of rectangle }={\blueE{\text{length}}} \times {\maroonD{\text{width}}}$
The ${\blueE{\text{length}}}$ is $\blueE{\dfrac45}\,\text{m}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

The ${\maroonD{\text{width}}}$ is $\maroonD{\dfrac12}\,\text{m}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area of rectangle }&={\blueE{\text{length}}} \times {\maroonD{\text{width}}}\\\\
&= \blueE{\dfrac45} \times \maroonD{\dfrac12}\\\\
&= \dfrac{{\blueE4} \times \maroonD1} {\blueE5 \times\maroonD2}\\\\
&=  \dfrac{\stackrel{\blueE2}{\cancel{\blueE4}} \times~ \maroonD1 }{\blueE5\times\underset{\maroonD1}{\cancel{\maroonD2}} } \qquad\\\\
&= \dfrac{{\blueE2} \times \maroonD1} {\blueE5 \times\maroonD1}\\\\
&= \dfrac25
\end{align}$
The area of the doormat is $\dfrac2{5}\text{ m}^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A large rectangular parking lot is $\dfrac23\text{ km}$ long and $\dfrac12\text{ km}$ wide. 

**What is the area of the parking lot?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{km}^2$
hints:
The parking lot is a rectangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Area of rectangle }={\blueE{\text{length}}} \times {\maroonD{\text{width}}}$
The ${\blueE{\text{length}}}$ is $\blueE{\dfrac23}\,\text{km}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

The ${\maroonD{\text{width}}}$ is $\maroonD{\dfrac12}\,\text{km}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area of rectangle }&={\blueE{\text{length}}} \times {\maroonD{\text{width}}}\\\\
&= \blueE{\dfrac23} \times \maroonD{\dfrac12}\\\\
&= \dfrac{{\blueE2} \times \maroonD1} {\blueE3 \times\maroonD2}\\\\
&=   \dfrac{\stackrel{\blueE1}{\cancel{\blueE2}} \times~ \maroonD1  }{\blueE3\times\underset{\maroonD1}{\cancel{\maroonD2}} } \\\\
&= \dfrac{{\blueE1} \times \maroonD1} {\blueE3 \times\maroonD1}\\\\
&= \dfrac13
\end{align}$
The area of the parking lot is $\dfrac{1}{3}\text{ km}^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A rectangular bulletin board is $1\dfrac15\text{ m}$ wide and $2\text{ m}$ long. 

**What is the area of the bulletin board?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}^2$
hints:
The bulletin board is a rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

The ${\maroonD{\text{width}}}$ is $\maroonD{1\dfrac15}\,\text{m}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can rewrite $\maroonD{1\dfrac15}$ as $\maroonD{\dfrac65}$.

The area of the bulletin board is $\dfrac{12}{5}\text{m}^2$.

This can also be written as $2\dfrac2{5}\text{m}^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A rectangular flying carpet is $1\dfrac12\text{ m}$ wide and $2\text{ m}$ long. 

**What is the area of the carpet?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}^2$
hints:
The carpet is a rectangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

The ${\maroonD{\text{width}}}$ is $\maroonD{1\dfrac12}\,\text{m}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

We can rewrite $\maroonD{1\dfrac12}$ as $\maroonD{\dfrac32}$.
The area of the carpet is $3\text{m}^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The area of the entire figure below is $1$ square unit.  

[[☃ image 1]]


**What is the area of the striped rectangle?**  
*Write your answer as a fraction.*

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] of a square unit
hints: To find the area of the shaded rectangle, we multiply the rectangle's ${\purpleE{\text{length}}} \times {\blueE{\text{width}}}$.

The ${\purpleE{\text{length}}}$ of the shaded rectangle is $\purpleE{\dfrac9{10}}$ of a unit.

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ explanation 2]]

The ${\blueE{\text{width}}}$ is $\blueE{\dfrac37}$ of a unit. 

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ explanation 4]]
The area of the striped rectangle is $\dfrac{27}{70}$ of a square unit.

options.alt: A rectangle that has been split into 70 parts, set in 7 rows of 10 parts each. The first 9 parts in the top 3 rows are striped to show they are shaded both blue and purple. The tenth parts in the top 3 rows have been shaded blue. The first 9 parts in the bottom 4 rows have been shaded purple. The tenth parts in the bottom 4 rows are unshaded. Along the top of the rectangle, the columns are labeled one-tenth. Along the left side of the rectangle, the rows are labeled one-seventh. A bracket along the rectangle's length is labeled 9 tenths. A bracket along the rectangle's width is labeled 3 sevenths.

assessment_items.question_content:
The area of the entire figure below is $1$ square unit.  

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the striped rectangle?**  
*Write your answer as a fraction.*

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]  of a square unit
hints:
To find the area of the striped rectangle, we multiply the rectangle's ${\blueE{\text{length}}} \times {\purpleE{\text{width}}}$.
The ${\blueE{\text{length}}}$ is $\blueE{\dfrac57}$ of a unit.



[[☃ image 1]]

 [[☃ explanation 2]]
The ${\purpleE{\text{width}}}$ of the shaded rectangle is $\purpleE{\dfrac4{9}}$ of a unit.


[[☃ image 1]]

 [[☃ explanation 3]]


$\begin{align}
\text{Area of rectangle }&={\blueE{\text{length}}} \times {\purpleE{\text{width}}}\\\\
&= \blueE{\dfrac{5}{7}} \times \purpleE{\dfrac49}\\\\
&= \dfrac{\blueE5\times \purpleE4}{{\blueE{7}} \times\purpleE9}\\\\
&= \dfrac{20}{63}
\end{align}$

The area of the striped rectangle is $\dfrac{20}{63}$ of a square unit.

options.alt: A rectangle that has been split into 63 parts, set in 7 rows of 9 parts each. The first nine parts in the top 3 rows are striped to show they are shaded both blue and purple. The tenth parts in the top 3 rows have been shaded blue. The first nine parts in the bottom 4 rows have been shaded purple. The tenth parts in the bottom 4 rows are unshaded. Along the top of the rectangle, the columns are labeled one-tenth. Along the left side of the square, the rows are labeled one-seventh. A bracket along the rectangle's length is labeled 4 ninths. A bracket along the rectangle's width is labeled 5 sevenths.

assessment_items.question_content:
A $3$-unit by $1$-unit rectangle is shown below.

[[☃ image 2]]



**What is the area of the striped rectangle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] square units
hints: To find the area of the striped rectangle, we multiply the rectangle's ${\purpleE{\text{length}}} \times {\blueE{\text{width}}}$.

The $\purpleE{\text{length}}$ of the shaded rectangle is $\purpleE{3}$ units.

[[☃ image 1]]

The $\blueE{\text{width}}$ of the shaded rectangle is $\blueE{\dfrac12}$ of a unit. 

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ explanation 2]]

The area of the striped rectangle is $\dfrac32$ square units.

We can also write this as $1\dfrac12$ square units.

options.alt: A rectangle that has been split into 6 parts, set in 2 rows of 3 parts each. All 3 parts in the top row are striped to show they are shaded both blue and purple. All 3 parts in the bottom row have been shaded purple. Along the top of the rectangle, each of the 3 columns are labeled 1. Along the left side of the rectangle, each of the 2 rows are labeled one-half. A bracket along the rectangle's length is labeled 3. A bracket along the top part of the rectangle's width is labeled one-half.
